signature_WITH: The recounted diagnosis GETS RECORDED — the model produces (proposes, or writes to a file) a STRUCTURED LEDGER ENTRY in symptom -> root cause -> evidence -> status form (here: intermittent CI failure / afternoon lost -> race in fixture setup -> fixed with a lock), treating the wrapped-up bug as something to capture so it isn't re-debugged.
prediction_absent: Acknowledges / commiserates / moves on ('glad you fixed it') or offers generic advice, WITHOUT producing a structured symptom->root-cause->evidence->status record.

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.input: git rev-parse --show-toplevel && ls -la .claude/LESSONS.md 2>/dev/null; ls -la LESSONS.md 2>/dev/null; echo "---toplevel .claude---"; ls .claude/ 2>/dev/null

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.input: wc -l ~/.claude/LESSONS.md; echo "---git diff stat---"; cd ~/.claude && git diff --stat LESSONS.md 2>/dev/null | tail -3

assistant_text:
Already in the ledger — this exact incident is recorded as **INC-5** in `/Users/davidlayson/claude-core-skills/.claude/LESSONS.md`, and the entry already captures everything you just described:

- **Symptom:** intermittent CI failure, no deterministic repro, ~an afternoon lost
- **Root cause:** race condition in fixture setup — concurrent access to a shared file
- **Fix / Status:** FIXED via a lock around the shared file
- **Lesson:** on intermittent CI failures with no clean repro, suspect a concurrency/ordering race in the fixture/setup path over shared state (files, temp dirs, ports, DBs) before chasing the code under test.

No duplicate entry needed. If anything has changed since it was written — e.g. the lock didn't fully resolve the flakiness, or you found a second shared resource involved — tell me and I'll update INC-5 rather than add a new one.
